rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AKIKO SPINDLE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) The 05/02/2022 filing does not address issue #2. The "amended account schedules" still does not have the correct unit amounts or estimated market value. For example but not limited to US Treasury Inflation Note 0.125% Due 04/15/2025 in the amended schedules alleges 900 units. The account statement shows a quantity of 90,000. The amended schedules show $96,858.90 market value in the account statement the estimated market value is listed as 102,852.53. There are many other entries in the schedules with the same issue. Please amend the account schedules and check each unit number so the units and values match what is listed in the account statements. "Amended pleading" means a pleading that completely restates and supersedes the pleading it amends for all purposes. "Amendment to a pleading" means a pleading that modifies another pleading and alleges facts or requests relief materially different from the facts alleged or the relief requested in the modified pleading. An amendment to a pleading does not restate or supersede the modified pleading but must be read together with that pleading. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GEORGE MATRANGA TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. Whether to sanction the parties/attorneys for repeated failures to follow court orders. At the 02/15/2022 hearing the court stated that this needs to be made a priority, otherwise it is going to send the parties to Dept. 206, that it wants to see in the amended petition what the statutory authority for the petition is, what the relevant legal standard is, what the material terms are of the settlement and what the parties think the effect of the court approving this settlement is. 2) The settlement proposed order does not match what is requested in the petition prayer/request.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VIRGINIA CAROLINE MARTINEZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/14/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: No courtesy copy of the proof of service filed on 1/28/22. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] No bank statements have been filed. [LRSF 14.34 D.] Receipts of refunds or reimbursements for payments made during estate administration are not includable in the fee base. Both claims appear to have been filed late. The creditors' claims period expired on 11/27/20. [Probate Code Sect. 9100.] No allowance or rejection of either claim appears to have been filed. [Probate Code Sect. 9250(b).] Why was the full amount of Daniel Myers's claim paid out of the estate, if the decedent, Administrator, and Delina Grace Martinez all shared in the life estate? Some of the court legal fees and filing fees (those paid on 6/14/21) are not adequately described. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 6/3/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF TATIANA NICHOLAS IVANOFF
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Grant without hearing. all reported acts and proceedings of petitioner as executor as set forth in the petition are confirmed and approved. Executor is authorized and directed to pay his attorney Raymond Chan the sum of $39,100.90 as statutory attorney fees for ordinary services. Executor is authorized and directed to pay his attorney Raymond Chan the sum of $445.00 as reimbursement for filings fee. Executor is authorized and directed to distribute the furniture and furnishings 50% to Alexander Eugene Ivanoff and 50% to Katherine Ivanoff Schooley. Any other property not now known or discovered which may belong to the estate or in which the Decedent or the estate may have any interest is distributed without further court order 50% to Alexander Eugene Ivanoff and 50% to Katherine Ivanoff Schooley.

rulings.Case Title: GUARDIANSHIP OF IVY HUANG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1) The guardian has been using a joint checking account under both names of the guardian and each minor to receive social security survivor benefits payable to each minor every month. The joint checking account is not a blocked account and does not earn interest. The guardian requests an order requiring the balance of each joint checking account and all future payments of the benefits to be placed in a blocked account to be established for each minor. 2) The ending date of the first account period is 03/24/2022 for Betty and 04/06/2022 for Ivy. The Court appointed the petitioner as guardian of the estate on 08/31/2021. Probate Code section 2620(a) requires the guardian to present her first account at the expiration of one year from the time of appointment, unless otherwise ordered by the Court to be more frequent. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review the Court's 11th Amended General Order which took effect on 04/25/2022 and LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YULIN LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/21/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Supplement is not verified by petitioner. All pleadings filed in proceedings under the Probate Code must be verified. If two or more persons join in a pleading, it may be verified by any of them. [CRC 7.103]. 2) Whenever an instrument written in a foreign language is offered for probate or attached as an exhibit to a pleading, it must always be accompanied by a copy translated into English. All translations must be accompanied by a declaration setting forth the translator's qualifications and/or credentials. [LRSF 14.15 D.] 3) Please provide the legal basis/cite probate code sections in support of Decedent already being married to Petitioner making the real property community property. The Grant Deed shows the property as Yulin Li, a single man. The owner of the legal title to property is presumed to be the owner of the full beneficial title. This presumption may be rebutted only by clear and convincing proof. [Evidence Code 662]. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/6/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF FREDERICO ALBERTO SILVA
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/22. Still no courtesy copy of the petition or of the proof of service filed on 3/8/22. It does not appear that notice was given to Peter Inwards or the alternate executors named in the will. The latter do not appear to be listed in item 8 of the petition. [Probate Code Section 8110(b).] None of the dates of death of the deceased contingent beneficiaries was set forth. [LRSF 14.18 D.] The supplemental proofs of service filed on 3/28/22 (which were not necessary, in any event: a proof of service of the Notice of Petition to Administer Estate is sufficient) all show that notice was mailed on March 5, 2021. Courtesy copies are file stamped, single-sided, hard copies and must be provided at least five (5) Court days before a hearing. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B.] Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 14.7 C.] A courtesy copy of the petition and the proof of service filed on 3/8/22, along with a supplement to the petition, signed and verified by the proposed personal representative, listing the alternate executors, and proof of service of notice to them as well as to Peter Inwards must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 5/27/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ANNE NOLE MARINO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues may include: 1) The will is dated 4/2/14 by Decedent but is dated 4/23/14 by the witnesses. The attestation clause states that the maker signed the will in the witnesses' presence. Petitioner alleges that the will was signed on 4/23/14, and the 4/2/14 date is a mistake. 2) Decedent died in 2014. Petitioner alleges that she was grief-stricken and became severely depressed for a number of years and was unable to open up this estate. She states that there are no issues with the assets of the estate due to the delay. 3) Petitioner stated in her verified petition that the decedent was survived by issue of deceased parents. However, she has since filed a declaration clarifying that the decedent was survived by her mother. Petitioner to address why said initial allegation was made under penalty of perjury. Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 11th amended general order.
